Title: Peripheral membrane proteins modulate stress tolerance by safeguarding cellulose synthases

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall structural polymer required for growth anisotropy and salt stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall of roots and hypocotyls
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose maintenance and salt stress tolerance by supporting CSC stability; loss enhances cellulose-deficiency phenotypes under salt stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (ttl1, ttl1ttl3, ttl1ttl3ttl4); complementation with pTTL1:TTL1-GFP; cellulose quantification; root and hypocotyl phenotyping; Y2H with CESA1-C
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly detected in the root elongation zone
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTL1 contributes with TTL3 to salt stress tolerance and maintenance of cellulose synthesis. 2. ttl mutants show shorter roots, swollen root tips, and anisotropic growth defects under NaCl. 3. TTL1 interacts with CESA1 catalytic domain through its N-terminal region and functions as a CSC-associated peripheral component.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm and plasma membrane-associated motile foci
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTL1 is a TPR thioredoxin-like peripheral membrane protein; categorized as Common Gene because it acts as a scaffold/stabilizer rather than an enzyme or TF
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve cellulose maintenance and salt tolerance without directly altering cellulose synthase catalytic genes
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tetratricopeptide thioredoxin-like protein with TPR repeats and C-terminal thioredoxin-like domain
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (100-200 mM NaCl); isoxaben phenocopy context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes stronger growth defects under stress; engineering effects on brassinosteroid/cell wall crosstalk may require caution

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTTL3 (native promoter, endogenous/complementation); 35S for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall structural polymer required for anisotropic growth and adaptation to salt stress
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall of roots and hypocotyls
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Safeguards cellulose synthesis under salt stress by stabilizing CSCs at the plasma membrane and promoting stress-resilient cortical microtubule polymerization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; complementation with pTTL3:TTL3-GFP; live-cell imaging with tdT-CESA6/YFP-CESA6 and mCh-TUA5; microtubule spin-down and turbidity assays; Y2H; Co-IP in Nicotiana benthamiana; subcellular fractionation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broad expression in roots and hypocotyls
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Broad in roots and hypocotyls; relocalizes strongly to plasma membrane CSC foci after prolonged salt stress or in prc1-1 background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTL3 is essential for maintaining cellulose synthesis and anisotropic growth under salt stress. 2. TTL3 directly binds microtubules and promotes/stabilizes their polymerization. 3. TTL3 colocalizes and comigrates with CESA6-containing CSCs at the plasma membrane. 4. TTL3 interacts directly with the CESA1 catalytic domain via its N-terminal IDR plus first two TPRs. 5. Salt stress or cellulose-deficient prc1-1 conditions trigger TTL3 relocalization from the cytosol to microsomal/plasma membrane CSC-associated foci.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm; peripheral plasma membrane-associated CSC foci; microsomal fraction
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTL3 is a new CSC-associated peripheral membrane protein and likely scaffold linking stress perception to cellulose biosynthesis; also previously implicated in brassinosteroid signaling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to improve cell wall integrity and salt tolerance by stabilizing CSCs rather than directly changing cellulose synthase catalysis; caution due to possible BR-signaling crosstalk
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tetratricopeptide thioredoxin-like protein with intrinsically disordered N terminus, six TPR repeats, and C-terminal thioredoxin-like domain
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (100-200 mM NaCl); cellulose deficiency in prc1-1; isoxaben phenocopy context
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropy through brassinosteroid signaling/cell wall process coupling; loss causes severe stress-sensitive growth defects

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall structural polymer supporting growth under stress
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor additional contribution to salt stress tolerance/cellulose-related growth phenotypes when combined with ttl1 ttl3
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Triple mutant phenotyping (ttl1ttl3ttl4) under salt stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTL4 contributes genetically to the TTL family effect on cellulose-related salt stress phenotypes. 2. Adding ttl4 to ttl1ttl3 only mildly enhances the phenotype, so it is not the main functional focus.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included genetically but not deeply characterized in Results; secondary core family member at most

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Primary cell wall structural polymer required for anisotropic growth and stress-resilient cell wall integrity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall of roots and hypocotyls
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary cell wall cellulose synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains that assemble into cellulose microfibrils at the plasma membrane
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key structural/catalytic component of the cellulose synthase complex; specific rate-limiting status not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Structural alteration or loss of CESA function causes cellulose-deficiency phenotypes; TTL proteins interact with CESA1 and stabilize cellulose synthesis machinery under stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid with CESA1 catalytic domain; co-immunoprecipitation with TTL3; genetic interaction with cellulose-deficient mutant context; live-cell CSC imaging
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CESA1 catalytic domain directly interacts with TTL3 and TTL1 N-terminal region in Y2H and Co-IP assays. 2. CESA1 is part of the CSC that is stabilized by TTL proteins under salt stress. 3. The paper supports a causal role for CESA-associated machinery in maintaining cellulose production under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CESA1 was experimentally probed as an interaction partner of TTL proteins, but TTL genes rather than CESA1 are the main study focus.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cellulose deficiency causes reduced anisotropic growth, root swelling, and hypocotyl defects
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress (100-200 mM NaCl); cellulose synthesis inhibitor isoxaben
